intro: Solea viscidula, also known as sticky sole or sticky sea plantain, is a type of succulent halophytic plant that grows in coastal regions and is often found in areas with high salt concentrations. The health benefits of Solea viscidula include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Solea viscidula is commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
